title: fix: use process groups to prevent shell timeout hangs on Unix
body:
This PR fixes issue #649.

### Problem
When `execute_shell_command` tool executes long-running or background processes on Unix (Linux/macOS), the current implementation only terminates the shell process itself. If the command spawned children that keep the stdout/stderr pipes open, the tool can hang or fail to properly clean up the session.

### Solution
- Use `os.setsid` as `preexec_fn` (on non-Windows) to start the shell in a new process group.
- On timeout, kill the entire process group using `os.killpg`.
- This ensures all descendant processes are terminated, releasing the pipe handles and allowing the tool to return gracefully.
- This behavior is now consistent with the existing Windows implementation which already uses process groups (`CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP`).

Closes #649

title: fix(skills): dedupe duplicated built-in skill listings
body:
## Summary
- fix duplicate skill entries caused by syncing active built-in skills with generated runtime artifacts into `customized_skills`
- dedupe the API/UI skill listing by skill name so customized skills override built-ins cleanly
- add regression tests covering runtime-artifact sync, real built-in edits, and deduped listing behavior

## Why this fix
`list_all_skills()` syncs active skills back into `customized_skills` before building the UI/API list. When an active built-in skill only differs by generated files like `__pycache__` or `.pyc`, the old directory comparison treats it as modified and copies it into `customized_skills`, which then makes the skill show up twice. This patch ignores runtime artifacts during sync comparison and returns a single logical skill entry per name.

## Validation
- `PYTHONPATH=src python -m pytest tests/unit/agents/test_skills_manager.py -q` -> `3 passed`

Closes #1370

title: feat: add MiniMax as a built-in LLM provider
body:
## Summary

Add [MiniMax](https://platform.minimax.io) as a built-in LLM provider. MiniMax offers high-performance models with a **204,800 token context window** via an OpenAI-compatible API, making integration seamless with the existing `OpenAIProvider` class.

### Changes

- **`src/copaw/providers/provider_manager.py`**:
  - Add `MINIMAX_MODELS` list with `MiniMax-M2.5` (default) and `MiniMax-M2.5-highspeed`
  - Create `PROVIDER_MINIMAX` as a built-in `OpenAIProvider` with `base_url="https://api.minimax.io/v1"` and `freeze_url=True`
  - Set `generate_kwargs={"temperature": 1.0}` to comply with MiniMax's temperature constraint (must be > 0)
  - Register `PROVIDER_MINIMAX` in `_init_builtins()`

- **`tests/unit/providers/test_minimax_provider.py`** (new):
  - Tests for provider type, configuration defaults, model list, manager registration, connection check, and model activation

### Model Details

| Model | Context Window | Input Price | Output Price |
|---|---|---|---|
| `MiniMax-M2.5` | 204,800 tokens | $0.3/M tokens | $1.2/M tokens |
| `MiniMax-M2.5-highspeed` | 204,800 tokens | $0.6/M tokens | $2.4/M tokens |

### Why MiniMax?

- OpenAI-compatible API — no new SDK dependencies needed
- 204K context window — ideal for processing large documents
- Competitive pricing vs GPT-4-class models
- Already referenced in `ALIYUN_CODINGPLAN_MODELS`; this PR adds direct API access

## Test plan

- [x] All 7 new MiniMax provider unit tests pass
- [x] All 60 existing provider tests pass (no regressions)
- [x] Provider correctly registered and discoverable via `ProviderManager`
- [x] Model activation works for both MiniMax models

title: feat!: Multi-agent/multi-workspace architecture (WIP - preview for feedback)
body:
# Multi-Agent/Multi-Workspace Architecture (WIP)

## Description

This PR introduces a **complete multi-agent/multi-workspace architecture** that enables running multiple isolated AI agents simultaneously, each with their own configuration, memory, skills, and tools. This is a significant architectural overhaul to support true multi-tenancy at the agent level.

### 🚨 Breaking Changes

**Configuration Structure:**
- The root `config.json` structure has changed significantly
- `agents` section now contains only references (`profiles`) to workspace directories
- Agent-specific configs moved from root to `~/.copaw/workspaces/{agent_id}/agent.json`
- Migration logic is provided but users should backup their configs before upgrading

**Data Directory Layout:**
```
~/.copaw/
├── config.json              # Global config (providers, active_agent)
└── workspaces/
    ├── default/             # Default agent workspace
    │   ├── agent.json       # Agent-specific config
    │   ├── chats.json
    │   ├── jobs.json
    │   ├── AGENTS.md
    │   ├── PROFILE.md
    │   └── skills/
    └── agent2/              # Another agent workspace
        └── ...
```

### ⚠️ Known Limitations & TODOs

This is a **work-in-progress** preview for community feedback. The following are **NOT yet implemented**:

- ❌ **CLI Commands**: Most `copaw` CLI commands still operate on global/default agent only
  - ~~`copaw init`~~ - needs update for multi-agent setup
  - ~~`copaw chat`~~ - needs agent selection flag
  - ~~`copaw skills`~~, `copaw tools`, `copaw channels`, `copaw cron` - need agent context
  - `copaw daemon` - partially updated but needs refinement
  
- ⚠️ **Agent Management UI**: Frontend agent switcher exists but needs polish
  - Bulk agent creation/import
  - Agent templates/presets
  - Advanced workspace management

- ⚠️ **Documentation**: Architecture docs and migration guide in progress

- ⚠️ **Testing**: Core functionality tested manually, but needs comprehensive test suite

### 🎯 What's Working

- ✅ **Complete workspace isolation**: Each agent has its own files, memory, chats, jobs
- ✅ **Tool & file operations**: Automatically scoped to agent's workspace
- ✅ **System prompts**: Each agent loads from its own workspace directory
- ✅ **Backend API**: All agent-scoped endpoints functional
- ✅ **Frontend**: Agent switching, config, skills, tools, workspace pages
- ✅ **Channels**: Messages routed to correct agent workspace
- ✅ **Memory & Context**: Per-agent memory compaction and management
- ✅ **Configuration**: Dependency injection pattern, no global state leakage

**Related Issue:** N/A (new feature)

**Security Considerations:** 
- Each agent workspace is isolated at filesystem level
- Tool guard rules and security configs are per-agent
- No cross-agent data leakage observed

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] New feature
- [x] Breaking change
- [ ] Documentation
- [x] Refactoring

## Component(s) Affected

- [x] Core / Backend (app, agents, config, providers, utils, local_models)
- [x] Console (frontend web UI)
- [x] Channels (DingTalk, Feishu, QQ, Discord, iMessage, etc.)
- [x] Skills
- [ ] CLI (partial - needs more work)
- [ ] Documentation (website)
- [ ] Tests (minimal coverage)
- [ ] CI/CD
- [ ] Scripts / Deploy

## Checklist

- [x] I ran `pre-commit run --all-files` locally and it passes
- [x] If pre-commit auto-fixed files, I committed those changes and reran checks
- [ ] I ran tests locally (`pytest` or as relevant) and they pass ⚠️ Many tests need updates
- [ ] Documentation updated (if needed) ⚠️ TODO
- [ ] Ready for review ⚠️ Preview only - feedback requested

## Testing

### Manual Testing Done

1. **Multi-agent creation**: Created 3 agents via frontend
2. **Workspace isolation**: Verified each agent has separate files/configs
3. **Tool operations**: Confirmed `write_file`, `read_file`, `execute_shell_command` use correct workspace
4. **Frontend switching**: Tested all pages refresh correctly when switching agents
5. **Chat persistence**: Messages saved to correct agent's `chats.json`
6. **Memory**: Verified memory compaction uses agent-specific thresholds

### What Needs Testing

- [ ] CLI commands with multi-agent setup
- [ ] Migration from old config structure
- [ ] Concurrent agent operations under load
- [ ] Channel message routing edge cases
- [ ] Cron jobs with multiple agents
- [ ] MCP client isolation (if enabled)

## Local Verification Evidence

```bash
# Pre-commit passed (with some acceptable warnings in legacy code)
pre-commit run --all-files
# Result: All hooks passed except legacy mypy warnings in init_cmd.py

# Backend starts successfully
python -m copaw.app.main
# INFO: Application startup complete.
# INFO: Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:7860

# Frontend builds without errors
cd console && npm run build
# ✓ built in 45s

# Core functionality smoke tested:
# - Created agents via UI
# - Switched between agents
# - Sent messages (saved to correct workspace)
# - File operations (wrote to correct workspace)
# - Config pages (loaded per-agent settings)
```

## Additional Notes

### Why Release This Now?

This PR represents **days of architectural work** and we want to:
1. **Get community feedback** early on the approach
2. **Enable parallel work** on CLI/docs by other contributors
3. **Demonstrate progress** toward true multi-agent support
4. **Avoid massive PR shock** - better to review incrementally

### Migration Path

Users on `main` branch will need to:
1. **Backup** `~/.copaw/` directory
2. Pull this branch
3. **First run will auto-migrate** to new structure
4. **Verify** migrated config at `~/.copaw/workspaces/default/agent.json`
5. **Test** basic functionality before creating new agents

### What's Next

Priority TODO list:
1. Update CLI commands to support `--agent-id` flag
2. Add agent import/export functionality
3. Comprehensive test suite
4. Architecture documentation

---

**This is a preview release.** Please test in a **non-production environment** first. Feedback, bug reports, and contributions are highly appreciated! 🙏

title: fix(console): fix copy button failures and model selector viewport overflow
body: ## Description This PR fixes two UI issues in the console chat interface: ### 1. Copy Button Not Working (Tool Messages & Code Blocks) The copy button in tool call messages and code blocks was broken due to two separate issues: **In `@agentscope-ai/chat` (upstream fix):** The `navigator.clipboard.writeText()` Promise was not handled — the success state `l(true)` was called synchronously before the async copy completed, causing the visual feedback to appear but the actual copy to silently fail. This is tracked in upstream issue [agentscope-ai/agentscope-spark-design#62](https://github.com/agentscope-ai/agentscope-spark-design/issues/62), which has been fixed in `@agentscope-ai/chat@1.1.53`. This PR upgrades the dependency from `1.1.51` → `1.1.53`. **In `Sidebar.tsx` (local fix):** The `CopyButton` component used `navigator.clipboard.writeText()` without fallback handling. In non-HTTPS contexts or older browsers, `navigator.clipboard` may be unavailable, causing silent failures. Added proper Promise chaining (`.then()` / `.catch()`) and a `document.execCommand('copy')` fallback. ### 2. Model Selector Overflowing Viewport The model selector in the top-right corner of the chat page used a CSS hover-triggered submenu that expanded to the right (`left: 100%`). On narrow screens or when the selector was near the right edge, the submenu would overflow outside the browser viewport with no scroll recovery. **Fix:** Replaced the hover-based flyout submenu with a click-to-expand accordion pattern. Provider rows now toggle inline expansion on click using React state (`expandedProvider`), and the panel uses `overflow-x: hidden` + `overflow-y: auto` to stay fully within the viewport. **Related Issue:** agentscope-ai/agentscope-spark-design#62 **Security Considerations:** None. The clipboard fallback uses `document.execCommand('copy')` on a temporary off-screen `

title: Feat/upstream/retry fallback history pagination
body:
## Description

This PR improves chat reliability and history loading behavior in two areas:

1. Graceful fallback for retry-exhausted transient upstream model failures
- When upstream model requests fail with retryable errors (e.g. 429/5xx) after retries, return a user-friendly bilingual assistant message instead of surfacing AGENT_UNKNOWN_ERROR.
- Improve retryable detection by parsing status code from both exception chain and exception text.

2. Paginated chat history loading (backend + console)
- Add `offset` / `limit` query params to chat history API.
- Return pagination metadata (`total`, `offset`, `limit`, `has_more`).
- Remove hard cap behavior that previously limited history to latest fixed count.
- Keep safety optimizations: hide low-value tool trace messages and truncate oversized plugin outputs.
- Update console session loader to fetch history page-by-page until complete.

### Why
- Avoid poor UX when transient upstream outages occur.
- Prevent large-history sessions from being artificially truncated while still protecting UI performance.

Related Issue: Relates to #1125, #1343, #1352

Security Considerations:
- No auth model changes.
- No permission model changes.
- No secret handling path changes.
- Existing output truncation behavior is preserved to reduce oversized payload risks.

title: Add send_file image upload support and integrate Fivemanage API for URL-based image requests
body:
## Description

This PR updates the runtime environment configuration of the web page and adds a send_file image upload feature.

Some APIs require a public URL instead of a local file path when sending images. To address this issue, this change introduces an image upload mechanism that uploads local files to an image hosting service and returns a URL that can be used in API requests.

Additionally, a Fivemanage image upload API has been integrated to handle the file upload process.

This improves compatibility with APIs that only accept remote image URLs and resolves issues when sending images using local file paths.

title: feat(skills): support importing skills from lobehub
body:
## Summary

- add `lobehub.com` as a supported Skills import source
- parse both LobeHub skill page URLs and direct marketplace download URLs
- download LobeHub skill zip packages and import `SKILL.md` plus bundled text assets through the existing skill creation flow
- update Console URL validation/localized copy and docs to list `lobehub.com` as a supported source

## Root Cause

CoPaw's skills import pipeline only recognized `skills.sh`, `clawhub.ai`, `skillsmp.com`, and `github.com`. `lobehub.com` skill pages point to zip package downloads on `market.lobehub.com`, but the backend had no parser or importer path for that source, so these URLs could not be imported.

## Solution

- refactor the shared HTTP fetch helper so the importer can fetch both text and binary payloads
- add LobeHub URL parsing for:
  - `https://lobehub.com/.../skills/<identifier>`
  - `https://market.lobehub.com/api/v1/skills/<identifier>/download`
- download the LobeHub zip package and extract `SKILL.md`, `references/`, `scripts/`, and extra text files into CoPaw's existing normalized bundle format
- prefer the skill `name` declared in `SKILL.md` frontmatter over the marketplace identifier when creating the local skill
- surface readable errors when the marketplace returns a JSON error payload or an invalid zip
- add unit tests for URL parsing, successful install, and invalid-version error handling
- update Console copy and docs to mention `lobehub.com`

## Validation

### Required local gates

```bash
cd website && npm run format
cd console && npm run format
pre-commit run --all-files
PYTHONPATH=src pytest
```

All passed locally.

### Focused checks

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src pytest -q tests/unit/agents/test_skills_hub.py
python -m py_compile src/copaw/agents/skills_hub.py
```

### Real import verification

I started the backend and console locally, then imported this real LobeHub skill through the Console UI:

- `https://lobehub.com/zh/skills/sickn33-antigravity-awesome-skills-data-engineer`

Observed result:

- `POST /api/skills/hub/install` returned `200`
- imported skill name: `data-engineer`
- imported skill was shown as enabled in the Console
- files were written to both customized and active skill directories in the isolated test working dir

## Notes

- the local UI verification used an isolated `COPAW_WORKING_DIR` to avoid touching existing user data
- the temporary local dev fixes needed to run the split frontend/backend stack (dependency install state and CORS env) were not committed as part of this PR

Closes #1314

title: fix(mcp): recover crashed clients automatically
body:
## Summary

Fix MCP client auto-recovery for issue #1308 by reconnecting clients after real stdio server crashes instead of only handling clients that already flipped to `is_connected = False`.

## Root Cause

`MCPClientManager.reconnect_disconnected()` only checked `is_connected == False`.
For stdio MCP servers, an unexpected child-process exit leaves AgentScope's client state at `is_connected = True` until the next protocol call fails with `ClosedResourceError`.
That meant watcher-based health checks missed the real crash mode and the agent could still fail during MCP tool registration.

## Solution

- add a protocol-level health probe in `MCPClientManager` using `session.send_ping()`
- rebuild and atomically swap unhealthy MCP clients from stored rebuild metadata
- keep the agent-side pre-flight recovery path for already-disconnected clients before registration
- let the watcher run the manager health check on each poll cycle
- add integration coverage with a real mock stdio MCP server and kill the child process to reproduce the production failure mode

## Validation

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src pytest -q
pre-commit run --all-files
git diff --check
```

All passed locally.

Closes #1308

title: feat: add optional web authentication with single-user registration
body:
## Description

Supersedes #519 — refactored based on maintainer feedback.

Add optional web login authentication to CoPaw. Auth is disabled by default and only enabled via `COPAW_AUTH_ENABLED=true`. Uses a web-based registration flow instead of environment-variable credentials, so agents running in-process cannot read plaintext passwords.

Key design decisions:
- Single-user model: one admin account per deployment (CoPaw is a personal assistant)
- HMAC-SHA256 tokens with 7-day expiry, zero new dependencies (stdlib only)
- Password stored as salted SHA-256 hash in `auth.json` under `SECRET_DIR` with 0o600 permissions
- Localhost (127.0.0.1 / ::1) bypasses auth so CLI access is unaffected
- CORS preflight (OPTIONS) passes through middleware
- WebSocket token via query param restricted to upgrade requests only
- First visit after enabling shows registration page; after registering, shows login page
- To reset password: delete `auth.json` from `SECRET_DIR` and restart

**Related Issue:** Relates to #519

**Security Considerations:**
- Credentials never stored in environment variables (agents cannot steal passwords)
- Auth token stored in localStorage, cleared on logout or 401
- `auth.json` written with 0o600 file permissions in `SECRET_DIR`
- Localhost requests bypass auth (CLI runs locally on 127.0.0.1)

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Documentation
- [x] Refactoring

## Component(s) Affected

- [x] Core / Backend (app, agents, config, providers, utils, local_models)
- [x] Console (frontend web UI)
- [ ] Channels (DingTalk, Feishu, QQ, Discord, iMessage, etc.)
- [ ] Skills
- [ ] CLI
- [ ] Documentation (website)
- [ ] Tests
- [ ] CI/CD
- [ ] Scripts / Deploy

## Checklist

- [ ] I ran `pre-commit run --all-files` locally and it passes
- [ ] If pre-commit auto-fixed files, I committed those changes and reran checks
- [ ] I ran tests locally (`pytest` or as relevant) and they pass
- [ ] Documentation updated (if needed)
- [x] Ready for review

## Testing

1. Without `COPAW_AUTH_ENABLED`: everything works identically to upstream, no login page
2. With `COPAW_AUTH_ENABLED=true`:
   - Visit web console → redirected to registration page
   - Register admin account → auto-login, redirected to chat
   - Logout via sidebar button → redirected to login page
   - Login with credentials → access restored
   - CLI via localhost → no auth required
   - Invalid/expired token → 401 → auto-redirect to login

```bash
# Docker test
docker build -t copaw-test -f deploy/Dockerfile .
docker run -e COPAW_AUTH_ENABLED=true -p 8088:8088 copaw-test
# Visit http://localhost:8088 → registration page appears

## Description

Add NapCat channel support for QQ bot integration via OneBot 11 protocol.

This PR adds a new channel implementation for connecting to QQ via NapCat, enabling CoPaw to receive and send messages in QQ groups and private chats. It also includes comprehensive documentation and bug fixes for group message routing.

**Why This Matters**

QQ is one of the most widely used communication platforms in China. This implementation enables:

- **Human-like Operation**: The bot can operate on QQ with capabilities approaching a real human user
- **Beyond Official Bot Limitations**: Unlike official QQ bot platforms with strict restrictions, this integration leverages NapCat's comprehensive API access
- **Team Collaboration**: Teams can leverage AI agents directly within their existing QQ workflow for group AI assistants, automated workflows, and knowledge base queries

**Related Issue:** Relates to adding new channel integrations

- Access token authentication support for NapCat API

title: feat(CLI): Add `copaw update` command to update CoPaw automatically
body:
## Description

Currently, CoPaw does not have a built-in version upgrade feature. Users need to use `pip install --upgrade copaw` to upgrade to the latest version. The community has reported several upgrade issues, mainly focused on the following aspects:

1. CoPaw is often installed in a virtual environment. Users may forget to activate the virtual environment, resulting in upgrade failures or upgrading in the wrong environment.
2. Users may not shut down the running CoPaw service before upgrading, leading to upgrade failures or abnormal service behavior after the upgrade.

## Solution

Provide a `copaw update` command to help users upgrade CoPaw to the latest version. This command will perform the following steps:

1. Check for the latest version of CoPaw
2. Obtain the virtual environment and installation path of CoPaw, compare the current version with the latest version, and if it is already the latest version, inform the user that no upgrade is needed
3. Check if CoPaw is running. If it is, prompt the user to shutdown the running CoPaw service, or shutdown with `copaw shutdown` command immediately
4. Start an independent update process to perform the upgrade operation. During the upgrade, display the progress and result. Use the installer (such as pip or uv pip) from the current CoPaw environment to ensure the upgrade is performed in the correct environment
5. Prompt the user to restart the CoPaw service to use the latest features


**Related Issue:** #569 #1195

The Console currently hardcodes all paths to `/`, causing assets 404, broken SPA routing, and missing images — making it unusable in most real deployments. The website frontend already supports `VITE_BASE_PATH`. This PR brings the Console in line. 3 files, ~5 lines. No new deps. No backend changes. Fully backward compatible. **Related Issue:** N/A (new contribution) **Security Considerations:** None — frontend-only path configuration change. ## Type of Change - [√] Bug fix - [ ] New feature - [ ] Breaking change - [ ] Documentation - [ ] Refactoring ## Component(s) Affected - [ ] Core / Backend (app, agents, config, providers, utils, local_models) - [√] Console (frontend web UI) - [ ] Channels (DingTalk, Feishu, QQ, Discord, iMessage, etc.) - [ ] Skills - [ ] CLI - [ ] Documentation (website) - [ ] Tests - [ ] CI/CD - [ ] Scripts / Deploy ## Checklist - [√] I ran `pre-commit run --all-files` locally and it passes - [√] If pre-commit auto-fixed files, I committed those changes and reran checks - [√] I ran tests locally (`pytest` or as relevant) and they pass - [√] Documentation updated (if needed) - [√] Ready for review ## Testing 1. **Default deployment** (no `VITE_BASE_PATH`): `npm run build` → serve `dist/` → Console loads at `/` as before 2. **Prefixed deployment** (`VITE_BASE_PATH=/copaw/`): `npm run build` → serve behind `/copaw/` → Console loads correctly with all assets and routing under prefix ### Default deployment (no prefix, backward compatible) ### Prefixed deployment (`VITE_BASE_PATH=/copaw/`) ## Local Verification Evidence ```bash pre-commit run --all-files # Changes are frontend-only (.ts/.tsx), not covered by Python hooks. title: fix(console): preserve custom cron expressions in UI
body:
## Summary

- preserve non-reversible cron expressions as `custom` in the console UI so hour ranges, lists, and step expressions are not silently downgraded
- add focused regression coverage for cron parsing in the console package
- serve the console SPA from `/console/` as well as `/` so the existing startup smoke test matches the current web console behavior

## Root Cause

The console tried to coerce any 5-field cron expression into simplified `daily` or `weekly` form fields by calling `parseInt` on cron fields such as `8-18`. That truncated range expressions to their first numeric prefix, so `0 8-18 * * *` was displayed as `daily 08:00` and could be rewritten to `0 8 * * *` when edited and saved.

Separately, the backend only served the console SPA from `/`, while the integrated startup test expected `/console/` to return the same HTML shell.

## Fix

- only map cron expressions into simplified `daily` or `weekly` states when the relevant fields are plain integers
- keep ranges, lists, and step expressions in `custom` mode so the raw cron string survives edit round-trips intact
- add `/console`, `/console/`, and `/console/{path}` aliases that return the same SPA index as the root console entrypoint

## Validation

- `cd console && npm test`
- `cd console && npm run build`
- `cd console && npm run format:check`
- `PYTHONPATH=/tmp/copaw-main-fPcYot/src pytest -q`
- `pre-commit run --all-files`

Closes #1253

title: fix(cron): inject dispatch channel into agent stream_query request
body:
## Description

Currently, the `CronExecutor` in `src/copaw/app/crons/executor.py` does not forward the configured `job.dispatch.channel` to the agent's `stream_query` context. As a result, when the backend agent framework consumes the request, the channel is missing, causing scheduled jobs to fall back to the generic `console` channel. 

This causes two major issues:
1. Messages are misrouted or incorrectly associated with the local terminal instead of isolated channels like Discord or Feishu as configured in the user's workspace.
2. Session histories tracking in `chats.json` get polluted cross-channel.

This PR adds a single-line injection to properly forward the `dispatch.channel` into the runtime request array, ensuring complete contextual isolation for scheduled jobs.

**Related Issue:** None

**Security Considerations:** Ensures that background agent conversations remain accurately scoped to their respective dispatch channels, preventing cross-channel session pollution in multi-user deployments.

title: fix(app): harden state storage against corruption
body:
## Summary

This PR fixes the state-storage failure behind issue #1217 and replaces the fragile runtime JSON state path with a more robust SQLite-backed default.

## Problem

The reported failure was a `JSONDecodeError` raised while listing chats after a local state file became empty or malformed. The immediate symptom was `/api/chats` returning 500, but the deeper problem was broader:

- `chats.json` and `jobs.json` were read without recovery when the file existed but contained invalid JSON.
- JSON writes used a fixed `*.tmp` path, which is unsafe across multiple processes.
- Session state used direct overwrite writes, so the same corruption class could recur outside chat metadata.
- Local verification was misleading because subprocess-based tests could import an installed `copaw` package from another checkout instead of the current repo.

## Root Cause

The runtime state layer did not satisfy the minimum storage guarantees needed for a local multi-process app:

- damaged files were not recoverable
- writes were not cross-process safe
- runtime metadata and sessions lived on ad-hoc JSON files without transactional guarantees
- test subprocesses did not consistently bind to the current source tree

## Solution

### Runtime state hardening

- add `json_storage.py` with recovery, backup, cross-process locking, unique temp files, and atomic replace writes
- harden legacy JSON chat/job/session readers to self-heal empty or malformed files instead of crashing the API
- guard chat history loading so bad session memory payloads degrade to an empty history instead of returning 500

### SQLite default state store

- introduce a shared `state.sqlite3` store for chats, jobs, and sessions
- add one-time migration from legacy `chats.json`, `jobs.json`, and `sessions/*.json`
- switch app startup and runner wiring to SQLite-backed repositories and session storage by default
- keep hardened JSON handling as the compatibility and migration safety net

### Verification reliability

- make pytest and subprocess integration tests import the in-repo `src` tree first
- add unit and integration coverage for migration, corruption recovery, API behavior, and startup flow
- update docs to describe SQLite as the default runtime state backend

## Validation

Executed locally:

- `pre-commit run --all-files`
- `pytest`

Key coverage added:

- corruption recovery for chat/job/session state files
- cross-process JSON/session write safety
- one-time legacy JSON -> SQLite migration and idempotence
- chat list/detail/create/update/delete behavior on SQLite
- startup and end-to-end HTTP flow using migrated legacy state
- subprocess test isolation to ensure current workspace code is what gets exercised

Closes #1217
